safe.title: Water within safe screening ranges
safe.summary: All screened parameters fall within recognised safe ranges for drinking water. This is a first-level screen and does not replace laboratory testing.
steps: Store the water in a clean, covered container.
Use a clean cup or tap to draw water to avoid recontamination.
Re-test if the water source, colour, smell or taste changes.

microbial.title: Suspected microbial contamination
microbial.summary: Cloudy water, low chlorine residual or high turbidity suggest possible microbial contamination. Boiling and disinfection are effective against microorganisms.
steps: Let visible particles settle, then pour off the clearer water.
Filter the clearer water through a clean cloth or household filter.
Bring the water to a rolling boil for at least one minute (three minutes above 2000 m altitude).
If boiling is not possible, disinfect clear water with household chlorine following local dosing guidance.
Store the treated water in a clean, covered container.

turbidity.title: High turbidity (cloudy water)
turbidity.summary: High turbidity reduces the effectiveness of disinfection and can hide microorganisms. Clarify the water before disinfecting.
steps: Let the water stand so heavier particles settle to the bottom.
Pour or siphon off the clearer water from the top.
Filter through a clean cloth, sand filter or household filter.
Then boil or disinfect the clarified water before drinking.

nitrate.title: Elevated nitrate or nitrite
nitrate.summary: High nitrate or nitrite is a chemical risk, especially for bottle-fed infants and pregnant women. Boiling does not reduce nitrate and concentrates it.
steps: Do not boil to try to remove nitrate or nitrite; boiling concentrates them.
Do not use this water to prepare infant formula.
Use an alternative low-nitrate source such as tested bottled or treated water.
Seek proper testing and, if needed, treatment such as reverse osmosis or ion exchange.

chemical.title: Suspected chemical contamination
chemical.summary: Suspected chemical, fuel, pesticide, industrial or heavy metal contamination cannot be made safe by boiling or household chlorination.
steps: Do not drink the water and do not boil it.
Use a different, known-safe water source.
Avoid using the water for cooking or preparing infant formula.
Report the suspected contamination and seek laboratory testing.

aesthetic.title: Aesthetic concern only
aesthetic.summary: Hardness or iron above the aesthetic threshold affects taste, smell or staining but is not a direct health risk.
steps: The water is acceptable for drinking on these parameters.
Hard water can cause scaling; iron can stain and affect taste.
A simple filter can improve taste and reduce staining if desired.
